feedback.details: mmdc validation exits 0 (verified at backend/c2-container.md:13-31). Contains exactly 7 container nodes: llm_switch (line 17), consul_agent (line 18), vault_agent (line 19), nomad (line 20), qwen_local (line 21), nemotron_local (line 22), frontier_api_gateway (line 23). All external entity (ai_app line 16) relationships use Rel() with protocol labels (HTTPS, Consul API, Vault API, gRPC). UpdateLayoutConfig present (line 31).

All required components present with correct C4 IDs. Minor issue: Uses Container_Ext for consul_agent (line 18), vault_agent (line 19), nomad (line 20) which implies external to technical boundary rather than containers within boundary. Standard C4 Container() should be used for infrastructure-as-containers within the llm-switch ecosystem. Also uses snake_case (llm_switch line 17) while contract references kebab-case (llm-switch).

Nomad HCL present (lines 41-94) with Consul health check at /health/ready with interval 10s/timeout 3s (lines 59-62). Vault agent config includes renewal=true (line 96). GPU resource present as device block: `device { name = "gpu"; count = 1 }` (lines 82-85) which is valid Nomad 1.0+ syntax equivalent to `resources { gpu = 1 }`. CPU (4000) and memory (8192) specified (lines 77-78). Minor deduction: Uses device block style rather than explicit gpu resource declaration.

OpenAPI 3.0 specification present (lines 106-756) with complete HTTP status codes (200, 400, 401, 403, 429, 500, 503) and rate limiting headers (X-RateLimit-Limit line 217, X-RateLimit-Remaining line 222). CRITICAL GAP: No curl examples present. Criterion explicitly requires "complete curl examples for GET/POST/PUT/DELETE with request body JSON schemas". Grep confirms zero curl examples in file. Missing securitySchemes section defining X-API-Key header authentication in OpenAPI components.

Section 760-769 cites technology-choices.md with section numbers (Section 1 lines 1-4, Section 2 lines 8-11, etc.). Go version 1.21+ specified (line 761). Docker base image gcr.io/distroless/static-debian11 cited (line 762). bifrost v0.4.0+ referenced (line 763). Rationales provided with benchmark references (OM-2024-004, HTI-2024-005, etc.). Minor deduction: File technology-choices.md not present in repository for verification.

YAML frontmatter present (lines 1-5) with author: Gerald, date: 2026-04-13, version: 1.0. Heading hierarchy correct: H1 (line 7), H2 (lines 11,34,38,96,103,759,772,786,800). All code blocks specify language: mermaid (line 13), hcl (line 40), yaml (line 105). markdownlint unavailable but structure follows standards. Minor deduction: markdownlint not available for validation.

Complete documentation (lines 773-783): Timeouts - LLM inference 30s (line 774), Consul discovery 5s (line 775), Vault operations 10s (line 776). Retry logic - 3 attempts with exponential backoff 1s, 2s, 4s (line 777). Circuit breaker - 5 failures in 30s triggers open state for 60s (line 778). Dead-letter queue with PagerDuty integration (line 779). Fallback mechanisms documented (line 780).

Complete specifications (lines 787-797): TLS 1.3 with cipher suite TLS_AES_256_GCM_SHA384 (line 787). mTLS for service mesh with 24h certificate rotation (line 788). API key rotation procedure with 90-day max age (line 790). Vault secrets path /secret/c2/* with ACL policies (line 791). Token renewal enabled (line 792). Authentication via X-API-Key header and OAuth2 Bearer tokens (line 793).

Complete constraints (lines 801-808): p99 latency SLA < 200ms under 1000 QPS load (line 801). Memory limit 2GB with OOMKilled prevention (line 802). CPU limit 4000 millicores with burst to 8000 (line 803). 100 concurrent connections per instance (line 804). Load shedding at 80% CPU (line 805).

overall_summary: The backend/c2-container.md document is largely complete but fails on API Endpoint Documentation Completeness (6.0/10) due to missing curl examples, which are explicitly required by the sprint contract. All other criteria are met with high quality: the Mermaid diagram validates successfully with exactly 7 containers, the Nomad job spec includes GPU resources and health checks, technology choices are properly cited with section numbers, and error handling/security/performance sections are comprehensive. The two minor issues are (1) using Container_Ext for infrastructure services when Container() may be more semantically appropriate, and (2) the file technology-choices.md is not present in the repository for cross-verification.
